concepts: compression release with retained intraday structure

thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. Live context still says the recent scored set is broadly non-promising outside one clear pass cluster, and the most repeated completed failure lanes are ETH-family refinement plus 4h and 1d event-driven exploration that keep dying on zero trades. The latest live exploit turn already consumed ETH/1h channel-shortbias, so chaining another exploit batch would violate the intended explore-heavy balance and worsen asset/family concentration. Stay inside the supported universe and rotate to DOGE 1h with 3 materially different but density-first intraday specs: one continuous value-hold continuation branch that stays active while price accepts above a fast/medium anchor stack, one failed-breakdown reclaim branch that turns on only after downside inventory is reclaimed and expansion starts, and one compression-release branch that enters only if structure is retained after release rather than requiring ceremonial breakout stacking. Do not clone ETH channel, ETH KAMA-stoch, or ETH 4h tail-harvester logic, do not recycle the recent BTC/TAO/DOGE 4h failed-breakdown grammar, and do not write narrow event stacks that cannot plausibly clear the 15-trade floor.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 15, and max_drawdown_pct <= 9.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.00, or max_drawdown_pct > 12.0.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: recent completed work is still explore-heavy overall, the latest live turn already used the only clearly evidenced ETH/1h exploit cluster, and DOGE/1h is a supported rotation that changes both asset family and timeframe away from the overused ETH and 4h/1d failure lanes.
